goal: Diagnose and mitigate PS2 disconnect and apparent restart behavior that appears while the robot is connected to the host for mapping/navigation.
deliverable: A diagnosis-backed firmware hardening change set with requirements, plan, code changes, and verification notes.
constraints: Preserve existing USART3 host protocol behavior.
Prefer minimal, local fixes over architecture-wide rewrites.
Do not assume hardware is the primary fault path because the user has already rechecked the hardware.
acceptance_criteria: PS2 communication becomes less sensitive to host-side serial activity.
I2C-driven IMU/OLED stalls are less likely to block the main loop long enough to destabilize control or trigger watchdog resets.
Changes remain traceable to concrete code paths and verification evidence.
product_acceptance_criteria: Host mapping/navigation sessions should no longer commonly induce PS2 invalid-mode warnings or apparent board restarts under the same usage pattern.
Existing ASCII/binary host commands on USART3 continue working.
manual_spot_checks: Connect host on USART3 and enable mapping/navigation traffic while repeatedly using PS2 controls.
Observe whether PS2 invalid-mode warnings disappear or drop sharply.
Observe reset flags and boot logs for absence of IWDG-triggered resets during the scenario.
completion_language_policy: Do not claim the field issue is fully resolved without code-level evidence and local verification. State residual hardware-risk uncertainty explicitly.
delivery_truth_contract: Only claim implemented mitigations and locally verified properties. Do not overstate hardware certainty.
non_goals: Full refactor of all blocking UART paths to DMA or queued transport.
Retuning IMU fusion parameters based solely on the connector change.
inferred_assumptions: The reported 'restart' can be caused by watchdog reset after long blocking paths, not only by power loss.
The plug-in MPU6050 improves signal integrity but does not remove the shared-I2C stall risk in firmware.
PS2 failures during host traffic are plausibly timing-corruption issues because PS2 is implemented by GPIO bit-bang without interrupt masking.
